reforms.description: The government funds more than 71% of total R&D spending and supports industrial technology by providing science and technology infrastructure, assisting industrial development in institutes and universities, and supporting the diffusion process of innovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for R&D and infrastructure enhances the capacity for innovation and industrial development, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government actively funds R&D and supports technology infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding and infrastructure, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government funds more than 71% of total R&D spending and industry 17%.

reforms.description: Tax credits for R&D expenditures have been on the rise during the 1990s in Mexico, aligning with trends observed in other OECD countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: R&D tax credits incentivize private sector investment in research and development, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Tax credits for R&D expenditures have been on the rise during the 1990s.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax credits represents a significant policy shift aimed at stimulating private sector R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: All OECD countries (except New Zealand) have special tax schemes for R&D expenditure, and most of them offer various types of R&D tax credits.

reforms.description: The Mexican government has initiated reforms to grant more financial autonomy to public research institutions, enabling them to develop research contracts with the business sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the capacity of public research institutions to engage with the private sector can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken initiatives to foster investment in knowledge and strengthen the innovative capacity of the economy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the governance of public research institutions, aimed at enhancing their role in the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The Mexican government is taking a number of initiatives to foster investment in knowledge and strengthen the innovative capacity of the economy.

reforms.description: Recent legislation has been introduced to improve the coordination of government R&D activities and enhance knowledge diffusion between public and private institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Better coordination of R&D activities can lead to more effective use of resources and improved innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation aims to provide for a better coordination of government R&D activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major step towards creating a more integrated and effective R&D ecosystem in Mexico.
reforms.source_quote: Recent legislation aims to provide for a better co-ordination of government R&D activities and mechanisms to enhance the diffusion of knowledge.

reforms.description: New provisions for fiscal incentives have been introduced to support R&D investment by the private sector, aiming to stimulate innovation and technology-based firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Fiscal incentives can encourage private sector investment in R&D, leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation includes new provisions for fiscal incentives to support R&D investment by the private sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is crucial for enhancing private sector engagement in R&D, which is vital for fostering a knowledge-based economy.
reforms.source_quote: It also includes new provisions for fiscal incentives to support R&D investment by the private sector.

reforms.description: The Electronic System of Government Procurement (COMPRANET) was developed to enhance the efficiency and transparency of public sector tendering, allowing for electronic submission of bids and public access to procurement information.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining procurement processes and reducing transaction costs, COMPRANET is expected to enhance public sector efficiency and accountability, ultimately supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The system became fully operational at the end of 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: The implementation of COMPRANET represents a significant improvement in public procurement practices, enhancing transparency and efficiency in government operations.
reforms.source_quote: COMPRANET has been developed by SECODAM as a universal, public-access system for carrying out public sector tendering.

reforms.description: The government proposed a series of reforms in February 1999 aimed at restructuring the electricity sector, including the establishment of a new legal and regulatory framework to facilitate private initiative in power generation and sales.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The proposed reforms aim to enhance competition and efficiency in the electricity market, which could lead to lower prices and improved service delivery.
reforms.status_evidence: The government proposed a series of reforms in February 1999, but these have not been approved by Congress.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed restructuring of the electricity sector represents a significant shift towards liberalisation and private sector involvement, which is crucial for enhancing efficiency and competition in a key economic sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government proposed a series of reforms in February 1999, but these have not been approved by Congress.

reforms.description: The ongoing reorganisation of the Comisión Federal de Electricidad (CFE) aims to create a 'shadow' energy market where all participants function under the same commercial principles, facilitating competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By restructuring CFE into smaller autonomous units, the initiative aims to foster competition and improve efficiency in the electricity sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The collective experience of other OECD countries shows that best practice reform of state-owned electricity sectors creates multiple generators privatised to separate owners.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reorganisation of CFE is a significant step towards improving the efficiency of the electricity sector, although it may not be sufficient to instigate real competitive behaviour.
reforms.source_quote: The ongoing reorganisation has started at CFE (approved by the CFE board in October 1999).

reforms.description: The 1999 Law to Enhance Scientific and Technological Research aims to improve coordination of government R&D activities and enhance knowledge diffusion between public and private institutions, including provisions for tax incentives for private sector R&D investment.
reforms.package_name: 1999 Scientific and Technological Research Enhancement Law
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing tax incentives and improving coordination, the law encourages private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant structural change in Mexico's approach to R&D, aiming to enhance collaboration between public and private sectors and stimulate investment in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The 1999 Law to Enhance Scientific and Technological Research provides for better co-ordination of government R&D activities and establishes mechanisms to enhance the diffusion of knowledge between public and private institutions.
